briefdescription.para: PS eFUSE Matrix.
Error Code Array.

This function is used to to write the Single eFUSE which checks for XADC temperature and voltage and verifies the written bit in the eFUSE Verification of the bit is done by reading the bit in margin 1 mode and normal mode.

detaileddescription.para: Temperature and voltage are read from XADC and verified for the correct range

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the eFUSE bit to be written
is the reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 60MHz specified in Hz

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_WRITE_VCCPAUX_VOLTAGE_OUT_OF_RANGE when voltage not in range
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_WRITE_TEMPERATURE_OUT_OF_RANGE when temperature not in range
Other errors because of internal functions and can be checked in xilskey_utils.h

inbodydescription.para: If the eFUSE bit is already programmed, no need to program again. That is taken care in eFUSE controller write function
Check the temperature and voltage
Write the eFUSE bit
verify only if programming is success verification of lower address is checked while higher address verification
Write the Redundant eFUSE bit address
if higher address is not success, check lower address and return
Successfully written lower address, so success
if higher address is not success, check lower address and return
Successfully written lower address, so success
Ideal case where lower and higher address is written properly

briefdescription.para: This function is used to write the RSA hash in the eFUSE Array in Little Endian.

parameterdescription.para: is the buffer containing the RSA hash to be written to the eFUSE array.
is the reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 60MHz specified in Hz

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.
Other errors because of internal controller functions and can be checked in xilskey_utils.h

inbodydescription.para: Convert the RSA Key hash to bit data
Prepare the hamming matrix for encoding the RSA Key hash
Encode the RSA Key hash with (31,26) hamming and write into the eFUSE array
Encode the data
Escape the Xilinx Reserved Bits
Write the eFUSE bit only if the value is one
Ready for next eFUSE Bit

briefdescription.para: This function is used to Read the RSA hash in the eFUSE Array in Little Endian.

parameterdescription.para: is the output buffer where RSA hash is copied from the eFUSE array after hamming decode.
is the reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 60MHz specified in Hz

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.
Other errors because of internal controller functions and can be checked in xeFUSEutils.h

inbodydescription.para: Prepare the hamming matrix for encoding the RSA Key hash
Encode the RSA Key hash with (31,26) hamming and write to the eFUSE array
Write 31 bit Row in eFUSE Array
Escape the Xilinx Reserved Bits
Decode the data
Repair the corrupt bit
Copy the data
Convert the bit data to RSA key hash

briefdescription.para: This function is used to read the PS eFUSE bit.

detaileddescription.para: Reading of the eFUSE bit is done in NORMAL read mode. Temperature and voltage must be in specified range for reading.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the eFUSE bit
is the reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 60MHz specified in Hz
is where the read data is stored. It will have values 0 or 1

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_READ_VCCPAUX_VOLTAGE_OUT_OF_RANGE when voltage not in range
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_READ_TMEPERATURE_OUT_OF_RANGE when temperature not in range
Other errors because of internal controller functions and can be checked in xilskey_utils.h

inbodydescription.para: Check the temperature and voltage
Read the eFUSE bit

briefdescription.para: This function is used to verify the written eFUSE bit.

detaileddescription.para: It is checked against the value of 1 only. Verification of the eFUSE bit is done by reading in MARGIN 2 mode which was highest margin.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the eFUSE bit
is the reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 60MHz specified in Hz

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.
XST_FAILURE an error occurred during verifying the PS eFUSE.
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_READ_VCCPAUX_VOLTAGE_OUT_OF_RANGE when voltage not in range
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_READ_TMEPERATURE_OUT_OF_RANGE when temperature not in range
an error XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_VERIFICATION when verification fails
Other errors because of internal controller functions and can be checked in xilskey_utils.h

inbodydescription.para: Check the temperature and voltage
Read the eFUSE bit in Normal mode
Read the eFUSE bit in Margin 1 Mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check the RSA Hash for all zeros.

parameterdescription.para: is the reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 60MHz specified in Hz

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if RSA Hash eFUSE bits are all zeros.
XSK_EFUSEPS_ERROR_RSA_HASH_ALREADY_PROGRAMMED if any RSA hash eFUSE bit is set
Other errors because of internal controller functions and can be checked in xilskey_utils.h.

inbodydescription.para: Set the controller to read in redundancy mode
Check the Hash eFUSE Bits for all Zero's
Escape the Xilinx Reserved Bits
Data value of 1 indicates Hash is already written
Set the controller back to single mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the controller mode to Redundancy/Single mode.

parameterdescription.para:
is mode of the controller to the set.
REDUNDANCY_MODE
SINGLE_MODE

inbodydescription.para: Configuration Reg - Redundancy Mode
Configuration Reg - Single Mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the controller read mode to Normal/Margin 1/Margin 2.

parameterdescription.para:
is the read mode of the controller
XSK_EFUSEPS_READ_MODE_NORMAL
XSK_EFUSEPS_READ_MODE_MARGIN_1
XSK_EFUSEPS_READ_MODE_MARGIN_2

inbodydescription.para: Read Configuration Reg
Reset the read mode
Set to Normal read mode
Set to Margin 1 read mode
Set to Margin 2 read mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check the read mode supported.

parameterdescription.para: is input read mode to be checked against

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if read mode is supported
XST_FAILURE if read mode is not supported

briefdescription.para: This function is used to write to the PS eFUSE.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the PsEfuseHandle which describes which PS eFUSE bit should be burned.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS.

In case of error, value is as defined in xilskey_utils.h Error value is a combination of Upper 8 bit value and Lower 8 bit value. For example, 0x8A03 should be checked in error.h as 0x8A00 and 0x03. Upper 8 bit value signifies the major error and lower 8 bit values tells more precisely.

simplesect.para: When called, this Initializes the timer, XADC subsystems. Unlocks the PS eFUSE controller.Configures the PS eFUSE controller. Writes the hash and control bits if requested. Programs the PS eFUSE to enable the RSA authentication if requested. Locks the PS eFUSE controller. Returns an error, if the reference clock frequency is not in between 20 and 60 MHz or if the system not in a position to write the requested PS eFUSE bits (because the bits are already written or not allowed to write) or if the temperature and voltage are not within range

inbodydescription.para: Check the variables
XAdc Initialization
Unlock the eFUSE controller
Check if the controller is unlocked
If eFUSE Array is write protected no more writes are possible
Configure the eFUSE controller with mode, strobe width values.
Enable Programming, write and read
Initialize the timer for delay while programming the eFUSE
Program the eFUSE based on the structure values
Program the eFUSE bit 0xA to enable ROM 128K CRC
Program the RSA key Hash value in eFUSE Array \
Check if all the hash eFUSE bits are zero or not
Program the RSA hash
Program the eFUSE bit 0xB to enable RSA authentication
Program the eFUSE bit 0x8,0x9 to enable eFUSE Array Write Protection
Once enabled the write protection, we will not be able to program any PS eFUSE afterwards
SLCR reset is required for write protect to take into effect
Disable Programming, write and read
Lock the eFUSE controller

briefdescription.para: This function is used to read the PS eFUSE.

parameterdescription.para: - Pointer to the PsEfuseHandle which describes which PS eFUSE should be burned.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.

In case of error, value is as defined in xilskey_utils.h. Error value is a combination of Upper 8 bit value and Lower 8 bit value. For example, 0x8A03 should be checked in error.h as 0x8A00 and 0x03. Upper 8 bit value signifies the major error and lower 8 bit values tells more precisely.

simplesect.para: When called: This API initializes the timer, XADC subsystems. Unlocks the PS eFUSE Controller. Configures the PS eFUSE Controller and enables read-only mode. Reads the PS eFUSE (Hash Value), and enables read-only mode. Locks the PS eFUSE Controller. Returns an error, if the reference clock frequency is not in between 20 and 60MHz. or if unable to unlock PS eFUSE controller or requested address corresponds to restricted bits. or if the temperature and voltage are not within range

inbodydescription.para: Check the variables
XAdc Initialization
Unlock the eFUSE controller
Check if the controller is unlocked
Configure the eFUSE controller with mode, strobe width values
Enable eFUSE reading only
Read the eFUSE
Read the RSA key Hash value from eFUSE Array
Disable Programming, write and read
Lock the eFUSE controller

briefdescription.para: This function is used to generate the matrix map of the G and H for hamming code (31,26).

detaileddescription.para: G is [31,5] and defined as [A|I], I is identity matrix of [5,5].
None TDD Cases: Check the generated matrix Check the memory corruption of the generated matrix

briefdescription.para: This function is used to encode the incoming data byte.

detaileddescription.para: It uses hamming (31,26) algorithm. 26 bits are encoded to 31 bits

InData
is 26 bit input data with each bit represented in one byte
Ecc
is the 31 bit encoded data with each bit represented in one byte
None TDD Cases: Check the parameters Check the encoded data for different input data Check the input data for boundary cases Check for memory corruption

briefdescription.para: This function is used to decode the incoming encoded byte.

detaileddescription.para:
Corrupt
is the input encoded data. It has 26 bit data with 5 bit parity data
Syndrome
is the output updated with the parity error information.
position of the error in the data byte TDD Cases: Check the parameters Check the decode with out any error Check the decode with 1 bit error Check the decode with 2 bit error Check the decode for boundary cases Check for memory corruption

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the controller mode, read mode along with the read and program strobe width values based on the reference clock.

parameterdescription.para:
is the mode of the controller
XSK_EFUSEPS_REDUNDANCY_MODE
XSK_EFUSEPS_SINGLE_MODE
is the CPU 1x reference clock frequency. Clock frequency can be between 20MHz to 100MHz specified in Hz

is the read mode of the controller
XSK_EFUSEPS_READ_MODE_NORMAL
XSK_EFUSEPS_READ_MODE_MARGIN_1
XSK_EFUSEPS_READ_MODE_MARGIN_2

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS no errors occurred.
an error when controller mode is not supported
an error when reference clock is not supported
an error when read mode is not supported

detaileddescription.para: Test Cases: Check single mode in CFG Reg Check redundancy mode in CFG Reg Check strobe width values for write mode Check strobe width values for various read mode Check Normal Read mode setting in CFG Reg Check Margin 1 Read mode setting in CFG Reg Check Margin 2 Read mode setting in CFG Reg Boundary Conditions

inbodydescription.para: Check the parameters Mode can be Single or Redundancy mode
Ref Clock should be between 20MHz - 60MHz
3 read modes are supported
Set the controller mode
Set the controller read mode
Program the Strobe width values for read and write 12us is required for write and 150ns is required for read PGM_STBW = ceiling(12us/ref_clk period) RD_STBW = ceiling(150ns/ref_clk period)

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check whether eFuse bit is xilinx reserved bit or not.

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the eFuse bit.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if address corresponds to restricted eFuse bit.
XST_FAILURE is address corresponds to non-restricted eFuse bit.

detaileddescription.para: Test Cases: with different address values Boundary values for addr

inbodydescription.para: Check for xilinx test bits
Check for xilinx reserved bits

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable the read/write/program the eFUSE array.

detaileddescription.para:
ReadWriteEnable
0x1 - Enable programming 0x2 - Enable read 0x4 - Enable write
Test Cases

inbodydescription.para: Reset the values Disable programming Disable reading Disable writing

briefdescription.para: This function is used to read the eFuse bit value.

detaileddescription.para: Before using this function set the controller mode and read mode as required. Also, strobe width values are to be set properly based on the reference clock for successful reading

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the eFuse bit.
has the read eFuse value stored in it.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS for successfully reading the value.
an error when addr is restricted

detaileddescription.para: Test Cases Read in Single mode Read in redundancy mode Read for restricted address Boundary Checks for address

briefdescription.para: This function is used to program the eFuse bit value.

detaileddescription.para: Before using this function set the controller mode and read mode as required. Also, strobe width values are to be set properly based on the reference clock for successful programming

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the eFuse bit.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS after successful writing.
an error when addr is restricted

detaileddescription.para: Test Cases Write in Single mode Write in redundancy mode Write for restricted address Boundary Checks for address Strobe width are not proper (Check if it makes sense)

inbodydescription.para: Check if Address is restricted
Send success when bit is already programmed
Providing 15us delay Timer takes 100ns as slice. 15us = 150 * 100ns

briefdescription.para: This function is used to read the PS efuse status register.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the PS eFUSE instance.
Buffer to store the status register read.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS.
XST_FAILURE

simplesect.para: This API unlocks the controller and reads the Zynq PS eFUSE status register.

inbodydescription.para: Unlock the eFUSE controller
Check if the controller is unlocked
Read the eFUSE status

briefdescription.para: This file contains the PS eFUSE API's to program/read the eFUSE array.

detaileddescription.para: Ver Who Date Changes 1.00a rpoolla 04/26/13 First release 1.02a hk 10/28/13 Added API to read status register.PR# 735957 2.00 hk 23/01/14 Changed PS efuse error codes for voltage out of range. 2.1 sk 04/03/15 Initialized RSAKeyReadback with Zeros CR# 829723. 3.00 vns 31/07/15 Removed redundant code to initialise timer. 4.00 vns 09/10/15 Added DFT control bits programming fecility for eFuse PS on Zynq. PR#862778 Added Conditional compilation to support Zynq Mp also. 5.00 vns 27/01/16 Fixed array out of bounds error CR #931207 6.0 vns 29/06/16 Added Margin 2 read verification after programming every Zynq's eFUSE PS bit CR #953052. vns 07/17/16 Fixed CR# 954260, Modified XilSKey_EfusePs_Write API to program eFUSE protect bit after programming DFT bits 6.6 vns 06/06/18 Added doxygen tags 6.8 vns 08/29/19 Initialized Status variables 7.2 am 07/13/21 Fixed doxygen warnings
